Petitioner San Francisco Fire Fighters Local 798, International Association Of Fire Fighters' Motion To Strike Return; Petitioner'S Request For Judicial Notice In Support Of Motion To Strike Return (Per Ruling Of 09-17-03 In 301)
Off Calendar Sep-30-2003 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Diane Sidd-Champion, Esq., McCarthy, Johnson & Miller, 415-882-2992, for petitioner/moving party San Francisco Fire Fighters Local 798.
Molly S. Stump, Deputy City Attorney, 415-554-4227, for respondents/opposing parties CCSF.
*ARGUED and the Court adopted its tentative ruling -- Petitioner San Francisco Fire Fighters Local 798, International Association of Fire Fighters' motion to strike return(re petition for writ of mandate); petitioner's request for judicial notice in support of motion to strike return (per ruling of 9-17-03 in D. 301) is granted. Petitioner is to prepare order. CSR: BELDON YEE, #2647 (REQ/302/EH)

MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: PETITIONER SAN FRANCISCO FIRE FIGHTERS LOCAL 798, INTERNATIONAL ASSOCIATION OF FIRE FIGHTERS' Motion To Strike Return; Petitioner'S Request For Judicial Notice In Support Of Motion To Strike Return -- Matter is transferred to Dept. 302 to be heard by Judge Quidachay. Moving party is to contact Dept. 302 to set a new hearing date. (301/JLW/EH) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558

ADDED TO LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR Hearing On The Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Sep-17-2003 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Diane Sidd-Champion, Esq. and Daniela I. Kraiem, Esq. of McCarthy, Johnson & Miller 882-2992, for petitioner San Francisco Fire Fighter Local 798 petitioning for writ of mandate
Linda M. Ross, Esq., CHIEF LABOR ATTORNEY, CCSF 554-4256, for defendant and opposing party

RULING:
A R G U E D and the Court took PETITIONER SAN FRANCISCO FIRE FIGHTERS LOCAL 798'S Petition For Writ Of Mandate under submission. The Court further heard petitoner's request for a further stay and ordered the stay in effect as is until 7/29/03. Petitioner can submit a prosposed order for a stay if there is no agreement reached by that day. (REQ/301/DR) Reporter: BELDON YEE, CSR #2647
